rationale: Both candidates independently found the exact latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling the removed get_cache_scale API, later issue #4891), which is the strongest signal of real investigation rather than diff-parroting. Y is more rigorous — it verifies the core design claim against actual upstream vLLM source (utils.py, fp8.py, weight_utils.py with line numbers) rather than just repo-local greps, and its test critique (the delegated-loader test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader so it never actually exercises the mapping it claims to guard) maps more precisely onto lishunyang12's real concern that the test doesn't validate true end-to-end behavior. X has slightly broader breadth (6 findings vs 3) but one minor finding is self-contradictory (faulting a comment for something X's own validated note already explained), denting precision. Both are highly actionable with concrete file/line references.
